curation.synopsis: On Aexe Cardinal, the Imperial Guard finds itself embedded in a planetary-scale stalingrad of trench warfare — a static Great-War-style front line where two Aexegarian factions, locked in a fifty-year-old grudge, are now joined by Crusade reinforcements on one side and Blood Pact warbands on the other. The Ghosts are dropped into this medieval gunfight as a scout-and-stealth regiment in a war that has barely heard of stealth. Gaunt's command intersects with the venal General Lyntor-Sewq and the local Aexegarian high command, where political snobbery toward an off-world regiment matters more than the daily attrition. Subplots: Trooper Cuu's killer-among-the-Ghosts arc tightens its noose around Larkin; Mkoll's scout teams probe the no-man's-land for ways past the Shadik trench network. The novel ends with a no-budge stalemate broken by Tanith reconnaissance feeding the artillery a working firing solution. Tone register stays in the trench-poetry vein throughout — somber and grinding.
